IND:Megatron-LM@8e64e69c5593->5a3aa174a9.mechanism: Commit #3789 added the condition `and (self.training or _CudagraphGlobalRecord.cudagraph_created)` to the `_should_call_local_cudagraph` check in TransformerLayer and MambaLayer, intending to force the EP dummy_forward to run eager. That condition also gated the CUDA-graph path in general: when `cudagraph_created` was False (and not training), the cudagraph path would be skipped and the layer would fall through to the eager path, losing CUDA-graph launch-overhead elimination. This revert (#3834) removes that faulty condition, restoring `if hasattr(self,'cudagraph_manager') and inference_context is None: return True`, i.e. re-enabling the cudagraph path. The correct version of the intended fix lands separately in #3815 using `not torch.is_inference_mode_enabled()`.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: expert model parallelism (EP); inference decode + CUDA graphs enabled

IND:Megatron-LM@b97184db5663->b8e23d5874.mechanism: The three forward_backward schedulers only called `off_interface.reset()` when `not forward_only`, so during eval/validation passes (forward_only=True) the PipelineOffloadManager singleton was never reset. `reset()` restores per-iteration offload state (`_cur_forward_chunk`, `_cur_backward_chunk`, forward/backward chunk indices set to 0, `_delayed_offload_groups=[]`, and `_cpu_tensor_pool.reset()` returning all pinned CPU tensors to the free pool). Skipping reset after an eval phase leaves the activation-offload manager with stale chunk state and CPU-pool tensors still marked in-use, corrupting offload behavior / leaking pinned CPU memory when training resumes. The fix removes the `not forward_only` guard so reset runs after eval too (and hardens the flag lookup with getattr default False).

manifest_conditions.parallelism: pipeline parallel (all three schedules) with MoE fine-grained activation offloading enabled; requires config.fine_grained_activation_offloading=True
manifest_conditions.hardware: GPU with CPU offload (pinned host memory)
manifest_conditions.trigger: an eval/validation phase (forward_only=True) interleaved with training

IND:vllm@0efd540dbc54->7e67df5570e2.mechanism: The Qwen3-VL dummy-inputs builder generated a profiling video with target_num_frames = get_num_frames_with_most_features(...), which for Qwen3-VL was capped at _MAX_FRAMES_PER_VIDEO = 2048 frames. This produces a single video encoder input whose vision-embedding size is enormous. get_max_video_tokens() also used this 2048-frame count to size the encoder-cache budget. In V1, if a single multimodal item's encoder outputs can never fit the encoder-cache budget, the scheduler cannot make progress and hangs. The fix pins dummy videos to num_frames=2 (target_num_frames = 2; get_max_video_tokens now calls get_num_video_tokens(..., num_frames=2)) and derives the max video-token size from the max-pixel image dimensions (get_image_size_with_most_features(max_pixels=video_max_pixels // temporal_patch_size)) instead of a 2048-frame video, restoring a consistent, schedulable encoder-cache budget.

IND:vllm@23d825aba11a->ea5f903f80fe.mechanism: Commit 23d825aba (#34392) disabled the AllReduce+RMSNorm+quant torch.compile fusion for DeepSeekV3 under NVFP4 quantization because the fused path produced incorrect results (issue #34395), forcing the model back onto separate all-reduce, RMSNorm, and quant kernels. This commit bumps FlashInfer 0.6.3→0.6.4 (which fixes the underlying fusion bug) and removes the DeepseekV3ForCausalLM config override that set pass_config.fuse_allreduce_rms=False, re-enabling the fused collective+norm+quant path and restoring the reduced kernel-launch/communication latency.

IND:vllm@2601f18a8216->14acf429ac08.mechanism: Async EPLB expert-weight transfers run on a background CUDA stream. The prior implementation recorded a CUDA event (`buffer_ready_event`) on the async stream after `transfer_layer`, and the MAIN inference thread called `stream.wait_event(model_state.buffer_ready_event)` on its current stream before consuming the buffer. This makes the main inference stream wait on the async EPLB transfer's completion, so a slow EPLB transfer stalls/bubbles the main compute stream — defeating the purpose of async EPLB. The fix removes the main-thread `wait_event` and instead does `cuda_stream.synchronize()` inside the async worker thread, so only the background thread blocks and the main inference stream is never stalled by a slow EPLB.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: expert parallelism (EPLB) with async rearrange enabled (eplb_config.use_async=True), MoE models e.g. Qwen3-Next

IND:vllm@3e440786afe7->02080179a3fc.mechanism: Under torchrun external_launcher + PP, broadcast_pp_output=True means PP outputs are already broadcast to all ranks at logits computation. Commit #32618 (async scheduling + PP) added an unconditional second collective: last rank calls torch.distributed.broadcast in _pp_broadcast_prev_sampled_token_ids while non-last ranks receive in _pp_receive_prev_sampled_token_ids_to_input_batch. In the SPMD external_launcher mode the ranks' participation in this extra collective is mismatched, so all ranks hang on the broadcast (deadlock). The fix gates the extra send with `not self.broadcast_pp_output`, skipping the redundant broadcast since token ids already reached all ranks.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: pipeline parallel (world_size>1) with external_launcher/torchrun (SPMD), async scheduling enabled

IND:vllm@41b92f7d38d3->92f0db57a881.mechanism: PyTorch 2.9.0+ disabled CUDNN's Conv3D backend, causing a significant slowdown of F.conv3d for the video patch-embedding conv. vLLM works around it in Conv3dLayer.forward_cuda by routing to _forward_mulmat (unfold + F.linear as a matmul) instead of _forward_conv when the conv is stride==kernel with no padding (enable_linear). This commit replaces the narrow guard `is_torch_equal(\"2.9.0\") or is_torch_equal(\"2.9.1\")` with `is_torch_equal_or_newer(\"2.9.0\")`, so the workaround stays active on all torch >= 2.9.0 (e.g. 2.9.2, 2.10) rather than silently falling back to the slow F.conv3d path on newer versions.

manifest_conditions.shape: Conv3d with kernel_size==stride, no padding, groups==1 (enable_linear); e.g. video patch embedding

IND:vllm@42135d689830->bfb9bdaf3f44.mechanism: The oracle FP8 MoE backend selector (`select_fp8_moe_backend` in oracle/fp8.py) tries backends in priority order (…VLLM_CUTLASS, TRITON, BATCHED_TRITON, MARLIN) and picks the first whose `is_supported_config` returns True. `TritonExperts.is_supported_config` gates on an allowlist `SUPPORTED_W_A`; the FP8 per-tensor-dynamic combos `(kFp8StaticTensorSym, kFp8DynamicTensorSym)` (standard) and `(kFp8StaticTensorSym, kFp8DynamicTokenSym)` (batched) were missing from the list, so Triton reported "unsupported" for those quant schemes. The oracle then fell through to the next available backend — Marlin, which repacks FP8 weights into a W8A16 mixed-precision format (`fp8_w8a16_moe_quant_config`, `prepare_fp8_moe_layer_for_marlin`), a generally slower path — or raised "No FP8 MoE backend supports the deployment configuration." Adding the two tuples re-enables the direct FP8 Triton MoE kernel.

manifest_conditions.dtype: FP8 (w8a8) per-tensor static weight + dynamic per-tensor/per-token activation
manifest_conditions.hardware: CUDA/ROCm GPU (oracle raises NotImplementedError on cuda/rocm if no backend matches)

IND:vllm@4e26d3b09e98->638a872d77b5.mechanism: `_set_compile_ranges()` was invoked before `current_platform.check_and_update_config(self)`, so the torch.compile compile_ranges were computed from a stale `max_num_batched_tokens`. On XPU with MLA enabled, `check_and_update_config` lowers `max_num_batched_tokens`, leaving the compile ranges (which are bounded by that value, per get_compile_ranges() in #24252) misaligned with the real runtime shape space — causing incorrect/extra compilation partitioning. The fix moves the call to after the platform config update so ranges reflect the final max_num_batched_tokens.

IND:vllm@574fe75245fd->999dfc1622d0.mechanism: In the async request path, render_cmpl/render_chat converted prompts to engine inputs with a synchronous list comprehension (`[self.process_for_engine(prompt, arrival_time) for prompt in tok_prompts]`), and the per-model renderers (deepseek_v32/grok2/hf) called `tokenizer.apply_chat_template(...)` directly. These are CPU-heavy blocking calls (tokenization, chat-template rendering, multimodal preprocessing) executed inline on the asyncio event loop, so under concurrent load one request's tokenization stalls the loop and inflates tail latency / TTFT for all in-flight requests. The fix adds `process_for_engine_async` / `_process_*_async` variants and `_apply_chat_template_async` that offload the blocking work to a shared `ThreadPoolExecutor` via `make_async`/`run_in_executor`, and dispatches them concurrently with `asyncio.gather`, keeping the event loop responsive.

manifest_conditions.workload: online serving (AsyncLLM / OpenAI API) under concurrent requests
manifest_conditions.path: chat/completions rendering, tokenization, chat-template apply, multimodal preprocessing

IND:vllm@59d53066d8da->a0638d052db7.mechanism: PR #32993 refactored get_accelerator_view_from_cpu_tensor, replacing the unconditional fall-through `return torch.ops._C.get_cuda_view_from_cpu_tensor(...)` with `elif current_platform.is_cuda(): ... else: raise ValueError`. On ROCm, is_cuda() is False (platform reports as rocm), so the UVA CPU weight-offloading path raised ValueError instead of taking the CUDA/HIP view path it previously used. This fix adds `or current_platform.is_rocm()` to route ROCm back through get_cuda_view_from_cpu_tensor (HIP shares the cudaHostGetDevicePointer runtime API in cuda_view.cu), restoring UVA-based CPU weight offloading — which the #32993 diff notes is required for cudagraph and reduces memory/allocation overhead.

IND:vllm@91f7d9d0b698->f85b4eda3a22.mechanism: The NIXL/UCX handshake runs in a background thread that has no active CUDA context. When device (GPU) KV buffers are used, UCX fails to find a valid CUDA context during handshake and silently disables CUDA IPC — thereby disabling NVLink communication and falling back to a slower KV-transfer transport. The fix calls current_platform.set_device(self.device_id) at the top of the handshake so the background thread has a valid CUDA context, restoring CUDA IPC/NVLink transfer.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: P/D disaggregation with NIXL/UCX KV transfer

IND:vllm@9eb58f8cf123->350ca72c0423.mechanism: Prior commit 9eb58f8cf (#32902) coupled AITER availability to the VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER env var: it changed is_aiter_found_and_supported() to also require `and envs.VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER`, and gated the ROCM_AITER_FA backend registration in eagle.py on `rocm_aiter_ops.is_enabled()`. Consequently, when VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=0, the optimized AITER Flash Attention backend module was not loaded/registered even when explicitly requested via attention_config, so vLLM could not use the AITER FA kernel path and fell back to a slower backend. This fix decouples 'can AITER work' (platform+arch+library) from 'should it be default' (env var), removes the `is_enabled()` gate in eagle.py (keeping only find_spec()), and adds un-decorated flash_attn_varlen_func/pa_fwd_asm entry points, restoring explicit selection of the AITER FA backend regardless of the env var.

manifest_conditions.env: VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=0 with explicit attention backend selection (ROCM_AITER_FA)
manifest_conditions.feature: attention backend / eagle spec-decode backend selection

IND:vllm@bbe0574d8e51->57a96e26c913.mechanism: Commit #33192 forced `can_use_trtllm = False` and `prefill_use_trtllm = False` whenever `vllm_config.kv_transfer_config is not None` (P/D disaggregation), disabling the faster TRTLLM attention kernel in the FlashInfer backend and falling back to a slower attention path. This revert (#34832) removes that gating, re-enabling TRTLLM attention for KV-transfer configs and restoring the faster kernel.

IND:vllm@c7914d30f90b->39f9ea0da4a4.mechanism: In pure `cudagraph_mode=FULL`, dispatch of a uniform-decode batch failed to find the registered FULL cudagraph key, so it fell back to PIECEWISE/NONE (no full-graph replay). FULL keys are registered via the mixed_mode loop with `_create_padded_batch_descriptor(bs, uniform_decode=False, ...)`, where `num_reqs = min(num_tokens_padded, max_num_seqs)`. At dispatch the old code built the descriptor with `uniform_decode=True` (giving `num_reqs = num_tokens_padded // uniform_decode_query_len`) and then only flipped `uniform` back to False via `replace(batch_desc, uniform=False)`, leaving the mismatched `num_reqs`. The lookup `batch_desc_to_check in self.cudagraph_keys[FULL]` therefore missed. The fix computes `normalized_uniform = uniform_decode and self.cudagraph_mode.separate_routine()` (False in pure FULL, which has no separate routine) before building the descriptor, so `num_reqs` matches the registered key and the FULL graph is dispatched. Default `FULL_AND_PIECEWISE` has `separate_routine()`=True so it was unaffected.

IND:vllm@d7982daff533->bfaa5593050e.mechanism: The reverted commit d7982daff changed the fused MoE Triton kernel stride arguments (stride_am, stride_ak, stride_be, ... stride_bbn) from default (int32) to `tl.int64` to avoid an illegal memory access (IMA) from int32 stride overflow. int64 pointer/stride arithmetic on GPU is more expensive (higher register pressure and more instructions per address computation) than int32, so it slows the MoE GEMM kernels. This revert restores the int32 strides, undoing that slowdown for the common case where strides fit in int32.

manifest_conditions.dtype: includes GPTQ/AWQ quantized (fused_moe_kernel_gptq_awq) and block-wise quantized paths
manifest_conditions.shape: matters when M/K/N strides fit within int32 range (no overflow); large tensors would require int64 for correctness (the reverted IMA fix)

Megatron-LM@43b4471d1eae.mechanism: Reverts feature PR #2738 ("Evict and re-compute context requests") in the dynamic-inference KV-cache scheduler. #2738 had split the KV buffer into equal active/paused halves (BlockAllocator: `active_count=(total_count-1)//2`, buffer `2*buffer_size_gb`) and made `is_memory_available` gate on `get_active_avail()>=num_blocks` (only ~50% of blocks usable for active requests) plus introduced eviction/re-compute of paused requests. The revert restores the prior model where the full buffer is usable for active requests (`is_memory_available` returns `total_avail>=num_blocks`) and a separately-sized `paused_buffer_size_gb`, plus `max_requests`/`max_total_requests`/`max_active_requests` naming. Since this is a revert, it restores the pre-#2738 concurrency/scheduling behavior — presumably because #2738 regressed or destabilized inference throughput/memory, though the revert message states no reason.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: dynamic-batching inference; pp_size>1 all-reduces block_count; tp_size rounds max_requests
manifest_conditions.hardware: GPU KV cache; unified_memory_level 1 uses CPU for paused blocks
manifest_conditions.shape: KV block buffer sizing (buffer_size_gb, block_size_tokens, max_sequence_length)

vllm@0118cdcc02ae.mechanism: compile_factors() builds the torch.compile cache key from env vars. After #26468 changed it from an explicit allow-list to an include-all-except-ignored-factors scheme, VLLM_OBJECT_STORAGE_SHM_BUFFER_NAME (a runtime-only shm buffer name for the multimodal object store, "only effective when mm_processor_cache_type == shm", which does not affect the compiled graph) started being hashed into the cache key. Differing values across workers/runs would produce different cache keys, causing spurious cache misses and unnecessary torch.compile recompilation. The fix adds the var to the ignored_factors exclude set; the added test asserts hash_factors(envs.compile_factors()) is stable across fresh subprocess initializations.

manifest_conditions.feature: torch.compile cache + multimodal shm object store (mm_processor_cache_type=shm)

vllm@517b769b5858.mechanism: In DeepEP high-throughput prepare/finalize under Dual-Batch Overlap (DBO), the DeepEP CUDA event that marks "this ubatch's compute is done" was captured AFTER dbo_yield_and_switch_from_compute_to_comm(). Because the yield switches to the other ubatch and queues its compute on the same compute stream, capturing the event after the yield made the event cover the other ubatch's work too. DeepEP honors previous_event as a dependency, so the dispatch/combine collective would wait on the other ubatch's compute, serializing the two streams and destroying the intended compute/communication overlap (GPU bubble). The fix captures previous_event before the yield in both prepare and finalize so the event only covers the current ubatch's compute, restoring overlap.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: DP+EP with Dual-Batch Overlap (DBO) enabled, DeepEP high-throughput dispatch/combine

vllm@57a96e26c913.mechanism: Commit #33192 forced `can_use_trtllm = False` and `prefill_use_trtllm = False` whenever `vllm_config.kv_transfer_config is not None` (P/D disaggregation), disabling the faster TRTLLM attention kernel in the FlashInfer backend and falling back to a slower attention path. This revert (#34832) removes that gating, re-enabling TRTLLM attention for KV-transfer configs and restoring the faster kernel.

vllm@ac3dac545b28.mechanism: The DeepSeek indexer fuses the WK and weights_proj projections into a single MergedColumnParallelLinear GEMM (wk_weights_proj). Prior commit 8617f8676 (#38870, "Fix DSV32 weight loading") gated this fusion behind `is_fp4_ckpt`, so non-FP4 checkpoints fell back to two separate GEMMs (self.wk + self.weights_proj) in the forward path. This commit removes the gate and restores the single fused GEMM for all checkpoints by dequantizing FP8 WK weights to BF16 at load time (_try_load_fp8_indexer_wk), keeping WK in a fusable dtype so the one-GEMM path is used again.

manifest_conditions.dtype: FP8 (float8_e4m3fn with weight_scale_inv) or other non-FP4 checkpoints; WK upcast to BF16
manifest_conditions.note: regression affected non-modelopt_fp4 checkpoints (FP4 kept fusion)

vllm@c5a66d16970f.mechanism: Under pipeline parallelism each worker owns only a subset of layers, but the auto-fit/memory-validation logic computed required KV-cache memory from the GLOBAL groups (all model layers) against min(available_memory) across workers. This over-estimated per-worker need, causing `--max-model-len auto` to shrink max_model_len (and hence usable KV blocks/context) far below what each PP stage's memory actually supports (or raise spurious 'not enough memory' errors). The fix adds `_project_kv_cache_groups_to_worker` and validates each worker against its own layers and its own `avail_mem`, so each stage is provisioned for its real shard.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: pipeline-parallel (PP>1), especially asymmetric per-worker memory or hybrid/uniform KV specs
manifest_conditions.shape: triggered on --max-model-len auto (-1) auto-fit path or the memory-enough check

vllm@f186cfe75e45.mechanism: The `_dummy_run` default `skip_attn=True` caused dummy runs (warmup / CUDA-graph capture / DP-EP sync) to pass `skip_attn_for_dummy_run=True` into `execute_model`, which took the `else` branch and set `block_tables=None, slot_mappings=None` instead of calling `prepare_dummy_attn`. For DeepSeek V3.2 this left attention metadata unprepared during dummy runs, causing a hang (attention/indexer path or collective mismatch). The fix flips the default to `skip_attn=False`, raises if `skip_attn` is True outside memory profiling, and asserts that FULL cudagraph mode must have attention metadata prepared for dummy runs.

manifest_conditions.parallelism: likely DP/EP (dummy_run also used for DP sync)

vllm@f9e6db30349d.mechanism: `max_seqlen` is a scalar computed on the host from a numpy `cu_seqlens` array (`MMEncoderAttention.compute_max_seqlen` returns a Python int). It is consumed by the ViT FlashAttention path (`_forward_fa`/`vit_flash_attn_wrapper` takes `max_seqlen`), which needs the integer value on the host to launch the varlen kernel. Wrapping the scalar into a `device=self.device` (GPU) int32 tensor forces a device-to-host synchronization (cudaStreamSynchronize) each time the value is read, stalling the CPU-side kernel-launch pipeline once per ViT forward (potentially per attention layer). Removing `device=self.device` keeps the scalar on CPU, eliminating the D2H sync.

manifest_conditions.attn_backend: FLASH_ATTN / ROCM_AITER_FA / TRITON_ATTN / FLASHINFER (paths that read max_seqlen as scalar)
manifest_conditions.hardware: CUDA/ROCm GPU (D2H sync only occurs with GPU-resident tensor)
